review_items.description: Verify generated script imports PYCFD_SRC_PATH correctly
review_items.findings: All 5 scripts use absolute PYCFD_SRC_PATH with sys.path.insert(0, ...) before import. Import of Solvers and run_pycfd_case follows path setup.

review_items.description: Verify config calls run_pycfd_case with valid dict
review_items.findings: All 5 scripts pass a valid _config dict to run_pycfd_case(_config). Config includes case_type, project_name, M_inf, aoa, gamma, rho_inf, CFL, second_order, use_limiter, inviscid_flux, eig_limiting_factor, t_final, dt, solver_type, flowtype, max_steps. MMS case additionally includes compute_te_mms.

review_items.description: Verify mesh parameters match case_type requirements
review_items.findings: Mesh parameters (mesh_type, mesh_nx/ny, mesh_xb/xe/yb/ye) were removed from the compiler config in favor of file-based mesh loading. run_pycfd_case maps case_type to case directory mesh files (.grid/.bc). Vortex→case_unsteady_vortex/vortex, Airfoil→case_steady_airfoil/airfoil, Cylinder→case_steady_cylinder/cylinder, MMS→case_unsteady_vortex/vortex (shared mesh), Shock→case_shock_diffraction/shock.

review_items.description: Verify flux and limiter selection is valid for flow regime
review_items.findings: All 5 cases use inviscid_flux='roe' (standard Roe flux for Euler equations) with second_order=True. use_limiter=False by default. Vortex (isentropic, M=0.3): second-order without limiter is appropriate. Airfoil (subsonic): appropriate baseline. Cylinder (subsonic): appropriate baseline. MMS: compute_te_mms=True for truncation error analysis. Shock diffraction: solver_type='explicit_unsteady_solver_efficient_shockdiffraction' is the specialized shock-capturing solver. Note: limiter may need enabling for transonic/supersonic cases with shocks.

review_items.description: Verify residual norm computation is present
review_items.findings: Residual norm computation is inside run_pycfd_case(), which calls _solver.compute_residual_norm() and returns L1/L2 residuals as JSON. The MHE executor parses these from stdout.

notes: The shock_diffraction case defaults to M_inf=0.3 in the compiler defaults, but the actual shock diffraction physics requires M_inf=5.09. The case mesh file and solver_type are correct; only the M_inf default in the compiler's _CASE_DEFAULTS table needs updating for this case.
The MMS case has no dedicated mesh files — it reuses the vortex mesh. This is acceptable since MMS verification evaluates manufactured solution convergence on any valid mesh.
use_limiter=False is a safe default for subsonic smooth flows but should be enabled for transonic/supersonic regimes.
